title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA achieved near real-time video diffusion generation by reducing the default 50-step denoising process through a combination of dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation. The approach stacks additive techniques where student models are trained to match teacher outputs in as few as one to eight steps, with gains of ten to two hundred times speedup.
writeup: Ziv Ilan presents NVIDIA's work on accelerating video diffusion, showing near real-time generation on a single Blackwell B200 by cutting denoising steps down from fifty. The speedup combines three techniques: quantization, caching, and distillation. On the distillation side, student models are trained to match teacher outputs in four, eight, or a single step, using either trajectory-based or distribution-based strategies. The takeaway is that the standard fifty-step count is not required, and step reduction plus quantization and caching can bring diffusion inference toward interactive speeds.
